Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a DuckDB SQL query based on a specific scenario Table Name event_log Columns case_id activity timestamp Logic Group events by case_id Count events per group Filter keep groups where the count is less than or equal to 6 Return all original columns rows for the kept groups

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify the Core SQL Concept This is a classic filter based on an aggregate condition applied to the whole set problem

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In SQL there are two main ways to do this Subquery or CTE Calculate counts first join back to the main table or use IN or EXISTS Window Functions Add a count column to every row then filter by that column in the outer query

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Evaluate Approaches for DuckDB Approach A CTE plus JOIN Standard SQL Pros Very clear standard SQL works everywhere Cons Slightly verbose

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Evaluate Approaches for DuckDB Approach B Window Function plus Qualify DuckDB specific optimization DuckDB supports the QUALIFY clause which is extremely elegant for this Pros Concise performant easy to read if you know window functions Cons Syntax is less portable to older databases

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Evaluate Approaches for DuckDB Approach C Subquery in WHERE clause Correlated Pros Intuitive logic where the count of this ID is Cons Can be slow on huge datasets if not optimized well by the engine

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Select the Best Solutions Since the prompt specifically asks for a solution for DuckDB I should provide the most idiomatic DuckDB solution first Window Function plus QUALIFY but perhaps also offer the Standard SQL approach CTE for clarity and portability

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining Option 1 QUALIFY Query SELECT FROM event_log QUALIFY COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id less than or equal to 6 Explanation COUNT OVER calculates total rows per case QUALIFY filters after the window function runs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining Option 2 CTE Query WITH filtered_cases AS SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT less than or equal to 6 SELECT e FROM event_log e INNER JOIN filtered_cases f ON e case_id equals f case_id

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Final Polish Present the answer clearly with code blocks and brief explanations of why it works The prompt asks for a query but providing the idiomatic one is best Self Correction during drafting Make sure to explicitly mention QUALIFY as its a powerful feature of DuckDB that makes this specific task much simpler than generic SQL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Output Structure Primary Answer Using QUALIFY Best for DuckDB Alternative Answer Using CTEs Best for understanding logic portability
